risk_notes: All npm dependencies use caret (^) ranges; none are pinned to exact versions, allowing minor/patch drift on install.
@vitejs/plugin-react is listed under dependencies instead of devDependencies in frontend/package.json, which is unusual and bloats production install surface.
frontend/package.json has an empty devDependencies object; build tooling (vite) is in dependencies, which is a configuration smell but not a direct vulnerability.
No lockfile (package-lock.json / npm-shrinkwrap.json) is referenced, so reproducible installs are not guaranteed.
Secrets (BROKERAGE_API_KEY, BROKERAGE_API_SECRET) are sourced from .env via dotenv — .env.example is committed with placeholders only, which is acceptable, but ensure backend/.env is gitignored.
No container images, no curl|bash install patterns, and no privileged-execution scripts detected.
